stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates relevance to modern, efficient model deployment practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core NLP/AI domain, specifically text generation using transformer models.
The tags indicate its relevance within the open-source AI community (transformers, llama, safetensors).

stories.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
The core of the dispute centers on OpenAI allegedly prioritizing profits over its original goal of benefiting humanity.
The high-stakes legal battle between Musk and Sam Altman could significantly impact the future direction of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: As Grok flounders, SpaceX bets future on beating Big Tech at AI
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: SpaceX is positioning itself as a major competitor to Big Tech in the AI space.
The company's IPO filing highlights orbital data centers as a key strategic advantage.
The narrative contrasts SpaceX's ambitions with the perceived struggles of competitors like Grok.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a generative AI.
Relevant technologies include transformers and safetensors, placing it within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/AI technologies, specifically text-generation pipelines.
The associated tags indicate the use of standard ML frameworks like PyTorch and Hugging Face components.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating active development and adoption.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents, enabling AI functionality directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is running AI agents locally, emphasizing user privacy and data control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files for operation.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: US government takes $2 billion equity stake in nine quantum computing firms
stories.author: Joe Miller and Michael Peel, Financial Times
bullet_points: The US government is investing $2 billion into nine quantum computing companies.
This represents a significant governmental push into the quantum computing sector.
One of the beneficiaries is a startup connected to the Trump family.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment.
MOSS proposes self-evolution by allowing source-level rewriting, moving beyond text-mutable artifacts like prompts and skill files.
The key innovation is targeting the core agent harness (routing, hook ordering, state invariants) for autonomous modification.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention mechanisms.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed memory states by decoupling erase and write operations.
Improves efficiency by reducing sequence mixing to linear time and decoding to constant memory.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: Introduces DeltaBox, a system designed to address the latency bottleneck in stateful AI agents.
Solves the problem of slow checkpoint/rollback (C/R) operations, which currently take hundreds of milliseconds to seconds.
Achieves millisecond-level C/R by efficiently managing the complete sandbox state (files, memory, contexts) required for deep search and RL.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment.
Proposes 'MOSS' for self-evolution by rewriting the agent's core source code, not just configuration files.
Highlights that current evolution methods neglect critical code elements like routing, hook ordering, and state invariants.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: The study systematically evaluates major commercial AI chatbots (Gemini, Grok, Claude, GPT series) as news intermediaries.
It addresses a critical gap by measuring how accurately these proprietary systems handle emerging facts across different languages and regions.
The evaluation involved 2,100 factual questions based on same-day BBC News reporting over a 14-day period.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation functionality.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: States ask judge to break up Live Nation-Ticketmaster
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Over 30 states have petitioned a federal judge to mandate the breakup of Live Nation-Ticketmaster.
The legal action seeks to force the sale of a significant number of large amphitheaters owned by the company.
Proponents also aim to limit the company's ability to tie access to its venues to its promotional services.

stories.title: Firefox is working on a rounded redesign with easy-to-find controls for privacy and AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Firefox is undergoing a major visual redesign, codenamed 'Project Nova', featuring a rounded UI.
The redesign prioritizes user experience by making privacy settings easier to locate and manage.
A key feature is the inclusion of a switch to disable all current and future AI functionalities within the browser.

stories.title: One of Meta’s big legal reckonings just ended in a settlement
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Meta settled a legal dispute with Kentucky's Breathitt County School District regarding the impact of its platforms on teens' mental health.
This settlement follows previous losses Meta experienced in related trials concerning youth mental health.
The school district had previously secured similar settlements from competitors like Google's YouTube, Snap, and TikTok.

stories.title: JWST maps the weather on a hot gas giant 700 light-years away
stories.author: Jacek Krywko
bullet_points: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has successfully mapped the weather patterns of a distant hot gas giant.
The findings suggest that current models used to study planetary atmospheres may be inaccurate.
This research highlights the capability of advanced space telescopes in exoplanetary science.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy (non-diverse) outputs.
Proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and support diversity required by inference-time search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
Focuses on improving LLM performance in novel environments and complex search scenarios by encouraging diverse response distributions.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework potentially unifies disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and temporal robustness.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The paper addresses the security and privacy risks associated with sharing latent communication (specifically KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems.
Latent communication via KV caches is noted as an efficient method for information exchange in complex LLM tasks.
LCGuard proposes a mechanism to guard or secure this KV cache sharing to prevent leakage of sensitive agent-specific or contextual information.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Introduces MambaGaze, a framework for real-time cognitive load assessment using eye-gaze tracking data.
Addresses key challenges in eye-gaze analysis: handling missing data (blinks/tracking failures) and modeling long-range temporal dependencies.
Potential application in safety-critical AI systems, such as driver vigilance monitoring and flight deck assistance.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-time wearable cognitive load assessment.
Proposes CogAdapt, a framework designed to adapt pre-trained clinical ECG models to the constraints of wearable sensors.
Focuses on improving cross-subject generalization and overcoming sensor configuration mismatches in cognitive load monitoring.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy responses.
Proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and handle diverse reward functions during inference-time search.
Focuses on improving LLM performance in complex, novel environments that require diverse outputs for effective search procedures (e.g., AlphaEvolve).

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness issues in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework potentially unifies disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and temporal robustness.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the security and privacy risks associated with sharing latent information (specifically KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems.
It proposes 'LCGuard,' a mechanism designed to act as a guardrail for safe KV sharing, improving efficiency while mitigating information leakage.
The work highlights the growing trend of using latent communication within LLM agents for complex task coordination.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: The paper introduces FAME, a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for log anomaly detection.
It addresses the limitation of existing systems that detect anomalies at a coarse session/window level, rather than the specific message level.
Message-level detection is challenging due to the ambiguity of event templates and heterogeneous subsystem failures.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard LLM training, which optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy (non-diverse) outputs.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and function effectively within complex, inference-scaling search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
The core contribution is improving the diversity of LLM responses, which is necessary for successful exploration in diverse, task-specific reward environments.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: LLMs demonstrate systematic and asymmetric political bias across different sensitive topics, termed 'covert political bias'.
The research identifies seven distinct categories of techniques through which this covert bias manifests in LLMs.
Two new metrics, Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency, are proposed to quantify and measure this political bias.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: Investigates how data ordering during pre-training affects the temporal knowledge acquisition of Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the current limitation where LLMs, trained on shuffled data, have poorly understood temporal grounding.
Introduces a new benchmark comprising over 7,000 temporally grounded questions for evaluation.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG addresses a limitation in existing biomedical knowledge graphs (KGs) by incorporating temporal information.
The research highlights that static disease associations fail to capture the dynamic nature of clinical reasoning over time.
The KG and associated benchmark aim to improve longitudinal clinical reasoning and retrieval augmentation in healthcare AI.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: The study investigates the 'Accumulated Message Effect' (AMEL), determining if prior conversation history biases LLM judgments.
Researchers tested this effect across 11 LLMs from 4 major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source models).
The findings are relevant to how LLMs are used as automated evaluators (e.g., code review, content moderation).

stories.title: Deepfakes Tore a High School Apart
stories.author: Brajeshwar
bullet_points: The story concerns the real-world impact of deepfake technology.
The incident involved the disruption of a high school environment.
It highlights the growing social and safety risks associated with generative AI.

stories.title: I Built a Browser SDK That Detects LLM Agents. Here's How It Works.
stories.author: devansh365
bullet_points: The article details the creation of a Browser SDK designed to detect LLM agents.
The core concept revolves around distinguishing between human users and automated bots.
The discussion touches upon the limitations of existing bot detection systems.
